name: Embalse de Arlanzón — circular by the foot of the Sierra de la Demanda
name_original: Embalse de Arlanzón — circular por los pies de la Sierra de la Demanda
region_intro: Jogging around Embalse de Arlanzón offers routes through diverse landscapes in the eastern part of Burgos province. The region is characterized by the Embalse de Arlanzón reservoir, surrounded by mountain scenery including hills and peaks of the Sierra de la Demanda. Many running routes follow the Sierra de la Demanda Greenway, an accessible path with a compact gravel surface. Joggers can experience varied terrain, from flat sections to gentle slopes, passing through oak and beech forests and along tranquil riverside vegetation. Best jogging routes around Embalse de Arlanzón The most popular jogging route is Embalse de Arlanzón — circular by the foot of the Sierra de la Demanda, a 6.5 miles (10.4 km) trail that takes 1 hour 17 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers continuous views of the reservoir and follows sections of the Sierra de la Demanda Greenway. Another top favourite among local runners is Pineda de la Sierra – San Millán (2,131 m) loop from Pineda de la Sierra, a difficult 18.5 miles (29.8 km) path. This route features significant elevation gain, leading through mountain terrain with views towards prominent peaks. Local runners also love the Tres Mojones – San Millán loop from Pineda de la Sierra, a 11.6 miles (18.6 km) trail leading through mountain forests and open areas, often completed in about 2 hours 47 minutes. Jogging around Embalse de Arlanzón is defined by reservoir views, the Sierra de la Demanda Greenway, and diverse forest ecosystems. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate circular routes to challenging mountain ascents. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 200 runners have used komoot to explore Embalse de Arlanzón's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the running trails around Embalse de Arlanzón?
A: The running routes primarily follow the &lt;a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Greenway_of_the_Sierra_de_la_Demanda" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de la Demanda Greenway&lt;/a&gt;, which features a compact gravel surface, ideal for running. You'll also encounter varied terrain, including flat sections, gentle slopes, and paths through dense oak and beech forests, as well as riverside vegetation.

Q: How many running routes are available in the Embalse de Arlanzón area?
A: Komoot offers a selection of over 25 running routes around Embalse de Arlanzón, catering to various preferences and fitness levels.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly running routes in Embalse de Arlanzón?
A: While many routes in the area are rated as difficult, there are a few moderate options available. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1050715556/embalse-de-arlanzon-circular-by-the-foot-of-the-sierra-de-la-demanda" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de Arlanzón — circular by the foot of the Sierra de la Demanda&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 6.5-mile (10.4 km) trail that offers continuous reservoir views.

Q: What natural features can I expect to see while running around Embalse de Arlanzón?
A: Runners can enjoy continuous views of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5192583" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arlanzón Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, stunning mountain scenery with peaks like Pico Mencilla and San Millán, and diverse forest ecosystems including oak and beech woods. The trails also feature picturesque riverside vegetation along the Arlanzón River.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural attractions near the running trails?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Greenway_of_the_Sierra_de_la_Demanda" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de la Demanda Greenway&lt;/a&gt; itself is a historical attraction, built on an old mining train line. Additionally, the renowned Atapuerca archaeological site, a UNESCO World Heritage site, is located in the vicinity, offering a significant cultural draw.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Embalse de Arlanzón?
A: Runners frequently praise the beautiful reservoir views, the well-maintained &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3678203" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de la Demanda Greenway&lt;/a&gt;, and the diverse forest ecosystems that provide a refreshing natural ambiance. The variety of terrain, from flat sections to gentle slopes, is also a highlight.

Q: Are there any circular running routes around Embalse de Arlanzón?
A: Yes, a popular circular route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1050715556/embalse-de-arlanzon-circular-by-the-foot-of-the-sierra-de-la-demanda" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de Arlanzón — circular by the foot of the Sierra de la Demanda&lt;/a&gt;, which is 6.5 miles (10.4 km) long and offers scenic views of the reservoir.

Q: What are the typical distances for running routes in this region?
A: Running routes in the Embalse de Arlanzón area vary significantly in length. You can find moderate trails around 6.5 miles (10.4 km), such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1050715556/embalse-de-arlanzon-circular-by-the-foot-of-the-sierra-de-la-demanda" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de Arlanzón — circular by the foot of the Sierra de la Demanda&lt;/a&gt;, up to challenging mountain ascents that can exceed 18 miles (29 km), like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20151441" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pineda de la Sierra – San Millán (2,131 m) loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Is the Embalse de Arlanzón area suitable for family-friendly running or walking?
A: The &lt;a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Greenway_of_the_Sierra_de_la_Demanda" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de la Demanda Greenway&lt;/a&gt;, with its compact gravel surface and generally accessible path, is well-suited for families. While many routes are challenging, some flatter sections along the reservoir offer a pleasant experience for all ages.

Q: What is the best time of year to go running around Embalse de Arlanzón?
A: The region offers beautiful scenery year-round. However, for running, spring and autumn provide pleasant temperatures and vibrant landscapes. Winter can be picturesque with snow-capped peaks, but some higher elevation routes may be more challenging due to weather conditions.

Q: Are there any specific viewpoints or scenic spots along the running trails?
A: Absolutely! Many trails offer incredible perspectives of the reservoir and surrounding mountains, including prominent peaks like Pico Mencilla and San Millán. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1754887" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Beautiful views over the Arlanzón reservoir&lt;/a&gt; highlight is a popular spot for panoramic vistas.

Q: Where can I find parking for the running routes around Embalse de Arlanzón?
A: Parking is generally available near access points to the Sierra de la Demanda Greenway and in towns like Pineda de la Sierra, which serve as starting points for many routes. It's advisable to check specific route details on komoot for recommended parking locations.
tips: It is one of the two reservoirs that supply water to the entire region of Burgos. If you are pedaling along the greenway, it is a perfect area to rest enjoying the views of the sheet of water and the spaciousness.

waypoints.name: Beautiful views over the Arlanzón reservoir
